question: Will QCOM's market close price on 2026-06-28 be higher than its market close price on 2026-06-21? Stock splits and reverse splits will be accounted for in resolving this question. Forecasts on questions about companies that have been delisted (through mergers or bankruptcy) will resolve to their final close price. Resolution criteria: Resolves to the market close price at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/QCOM for the resolution date. If the resolution date coincides with a day the market is closed (weekend, holiday, etc.) the previous market close price is used. Background: QUALCOMM Incorporated engages in the development and commercialization of foundational technologies for the wireless industry worldwide. It operates through three segments: Qualcomm CDMA Technologies (QCT); Qualcomm Technology Licensing (QTL); and Qualcomm Strategic Initiatives (QSI). The QCT segment develops and supplies integrated circuits and system software with connectivity and computing technologies for use in mobile devices; automotive systems for connectivity, digital cockpit, and ADAS/AD; and IoT, including consumer electronic devices, industrial devices, and edge networking products. The QTL segment grants licenses or provides rights to use portions of its intellectual property portfolio, which include various patent rights useful in the manufacture and sale of wireless products comprising products implementing LTE, and/or OFDMA-based 5G products and derivatives; to use cellular standard-essential patents, including 3G, 4G and 5G for cellular devices. The QSI segment invests in early-stage companies in various industries, including 5G, artificial intelligence, automotive, consumer, enterprise, cloud, IoT, and extended reality, and investments, including non-marketable equity securities and, to a lesser extent, marketable equity securities, and convertible debt instruments. It also provides development, and other services and sells related products to the United States government agencies and their contractors. In addition, the company is also involved in Qualcomm government technologies and data center businesses. QUALCOMM Incorporated was incorporated in 1985 and is headquartered in San Diego, California.

Background: QUALCOMM Incorporated engages in the development and commercialization of foundational technologies for the wireless industry worldwide. It operates through three segments: Qualcomm CDMA Technologies (QCT); Qualcomm Technology Licensing (QTL); and Qualcomm Strategic Initiatives (QSI). The QCT segment develops and supplies integrated circuits and system software with connectivity and computing technologies for use in mobile devices; automotive systems for connectivity, digital cockpit, and ADAS/AD; and IoT, including consumer electronic devices, industrial devices, and edge networking products. The QTL segment grants licenses or provides rights to use portions of its intellectual property portfolio, which include various patent rights useful in the manufacture and sale of wireless products comprising products implementing LTE, and/or OFDMA-based 5G products and derivatives; to use cellular standard-essential patents, including 3G, 4G and 5G for cellular devices. The QSI segment invests in early-stage companies in various industries, including 5G, artificial intelligence, automotive, consumer, enterprise, cloud, IoT, and extended reality, and investments, including non-marketable equity securities and, to a lesser extent, marketable equity securities, and convertible debt instruments. evidences.title: QUALCOMM Incorporated (QCOM) Stock Split History | Bullfincher
evidences.content: logo # QUALCOMM Incorporated (QCOM) Stock Split History QUALCOMM Incorporated (QCOM) has split its stock four times since going public, reflecting periods of strong long-term growth and rising share prices. QUALCOMM Incorporated’s most recent stock split was a 2-for-1 split on August 16, 2004, following a 4-for-1 split on December 31, 1999. Earlier, QUALCOMM Incorporated executed 2-for-1 stock splits on May 11, 1999, and 2-for-1 stock splits on February 24, 1994. Stock splits do not change QUALCOMM Incorporated’s total market value, but they increase the number of shares outstanding and reduce the per-share price, making the stock more accessible to investors. evidences.title: Mobile Phone Semiconductor Market Report, Industry and Market Size & Revenue, Share, Forecast 2024–2030
evidences.content: ## Opportunities Rising demand for AI-enabled smartphones The rapid integration of artificial intelligence features such as real-time language processing, image enhancement, and generative AI tools is increasing demand for powerful mobile processors and dedicated AI acceleration hardware. Expansion of 5G and future wireless technologies The continued rollout of 5G networks and early development of next-generation wireless technologies will require more advanced RF modules, modems, and connectivity chips in smartphones. Growth of premium smartphone segments Consumers increasingly demand high-performance devices with advanced gaming, imaging, and AI capabilities, which significantly increases the semiconductor content per smartphone. ## Restraints [...] AI-Centric Chip Architecture Artificial intelligence has moved from the cloud directly onto smartphones. This shift is pushing semiconductor designers to integrate dedicated AI engines or Neural Processing Units (NPUs) within system-on-chip ( SoC ) architectures. These AI cores enable real-time processing for features such as: Voice assistants and speech recognition Computational photography and image enhancement Real-time translat...

evidences.content: Akash Palkhiwala Chief Financial Officer, QUALCOMM, Inc. A Sustainability. So on operating margins, as you can see, we made tremendous progress over the last two years, right? We've gone from 15% to 29%, we'd set a target of 20% plus two years ago, and we beat it by a wide margin. So super happy with that. We think a prudent target to set going forward is 30% plus. This is I think a reasonable way to operate the company going forward, and we might have opportunities to go higher than that. The real question is what we're trying to contemplate in our guidance is a normalized supply chain environment as well. So we think at this point, this is a reasonable guidance point. evidences.title: did qualcomm stock split history
evidences.content: Did Qualcomm stock split appears throughout this article to make the timeline and verification steps easy to find for investors and researchers. ## Summary answer Short answer: yes. Did Qualcomm stock split? Qualcomm has executed four stock splits in its public history. The splits were: 2-for-1 in February 1994 2-for-1 in May 1999 4-for-1 in December 1999 2-for-1 in August 2004 These four splits compound to a cumulative split factor of 32-to-1 (2 × 2 × 4 × 2 = 32). The most recent split was the 2-for-1 split effective in mid-August 2004. This confirms that if you held a single pre-1994 Qualcomm share, it would convert into 32 shares after all splits were applied. [...] ### 1999 (May) — 2-for-1 split (May 1999) Did Qualcomm stock split in May 1999? Yes. Qualcomm announced and executed a 2-for-1 split in May 1999. This was one of two splits in 1999, reflecting strong market performance and a common corporate approach to maintain share price accessibility during rapid appreciation. ### 1999 (December) — 4-for-1 split (December 1999) Did Qualcomm stock split again in December 1999? Yes. In December 1999 Qualcomm implemented a 4-for-1 split after shareholder approval; the company iss...

evidences.title: Qualcomm - About - FAQ
evidences.content: Qualcomm has had four stock splits since going public. February 16, 1994 2 for 1 @ $86 per share May 10, 1999 2 for 1 @ $212 per share December 30, 1999 4 for 1 @ $647 per share August 13, 2004 2 for 1 @ $69.17 per share ## What are Qualcomm's fiscal quarters? Fiscal quarters for fiscal year 2025 are: First Quarter: September 30, 2024 - December 29, 2024 Second Quarter: December 30, 2024 - March 30, 2025 Third Quarter: March 31, 2025 - June 29, 2025 Fourth Quarter: June 30, 2025 - September 28, 2025 Fiscal quarters for fiscal year 2026 are: First Quarter: September 29, 2025 - December 28, 2025 Second Quarter: December 29, 2025 - March 29, 2026 Third Quarter: March 30, 2026 - June 28, 2026 Fourth Quarter: June 29, 2026 - September 27, 2026 [...] # FAQ ## How long has Qualcomm been in business? Qualcomm was founded in July of 1985. Qualcomm History (opens in new tab) ## Where is Qualcomm located? Qualcomm Incorporated Headquarters is located at: 5775 Morehouse Drive San Diego, CA 92121 ## Which exchange is Qualcomm stock traded on and what is the ticker symbol? Qualcomm is listed on the NASDAQ under the symbol QCOM. Stock Information ## When were Qualcomm's Public Offerings? Data...

Please note that the dividend history might include the company’s preferred securities as well.Price/Earnings Ratio is a widely used stock evaluation measure. For a security, the Price/Earnings Ratio is given by dividing the Last Sale Price by the Actual EPS (Earnings Per Share). evidences.content: Components of an Earnings Trade background background Earnings Whispers is the most trusted source for real earnings expectations - relied on by more people than any other earnings-dedicated website. The Wall Street Journal says EarningsWhispers is the only provider of real whisper numbers from securities analysts. A Bloomberg News study found EarningsWhispers to be the most accurate source fo earnings expectations. Academic studies show trading on EarningsWhispers data significantly outperforms the overall market. Barron's says EarningsWhispers is their favorite source for earnings information. Michael Lewis, bestselling author of Flash Boys, Liar's Poker, The Big Short, & The Blindside said EarningsWhispers is more reliable than the most highly paid analyst on Wall Street.
